mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market weakness signals impending recessionary pressures, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20d returns with a t-stat > 2.0 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rebound in industrial production following a sharp contraction signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, leading to positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns during post-shock recovery periods show no statistically significant positive drift (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following high inflation prints are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and yields positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows zero predictive power for 20d returns (coefficient p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.0 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, dragging down related sectors and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during low housing start regimes are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03.
